name: Mauritius

colors.symbolism: the struggle for freedom and independence
the Indian Ocean surrounding the islands
the new light of independence shining over the country
the lush vegetation and year-round agriculture of the islands

description: Four equal horizontal stripes of red, blue, yellow, and green from top to bottom.
history: Adopted on 12 March 1968 when Mauritius gained independence from the United Kingdom. Designed by Gurudutt Moher, the flag is one of the very few national flags with four horizontal stripes and unusually has no charge or device.
